origin.name: Serbia
origin.key_document: Izvod iz maticne knjige umrlih (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Serbia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Belgrade guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Serbia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the death certificate, cremation certificate, and export documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Izvod iz maticne knjige umrlih (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Serbia is a Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered at the local civil registry office promptly.
All Serbian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Serbian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Belgrade can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Serbia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Serbia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil registry, apostille, and Serbian translation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
